# S003 Run 023

## Metadata

- Scenario ID: `S003`
- Scenario: `S003-async-gpu-remote-node-image`
- Status: fixture-readiness-rerun-with-findings
- Acceptance level: not fixture-ready
- Coordinator: Codex
- Executed: June 6, 2026
- Workspace: `contract-sim/workspaces/S003/`
- Scope: full bounded fixture-readiness rerun after the validated post-Run 022
  contract/fixture alignment patch.

## Purpose

Test whether the post-Run 022 patch made S003 fixture-ready. All actors
received exact allowed file paths and were told not to use broad filesystem
discovery.

## Actor Verdicts

| Actor | Verdict | Summary |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `agent-user` | `fixture-ready` | Public context hygiene, public aliases, replay fixtures, idempotency, cursors, and public binary fixture all pass. |
| `c03-service-catalog` | `fixture-ready` | Catalog list/detail/filter/route fixtures remain aligned and bounded. |
| `c04-agent-tool-gateway` | `fixture-ready` | Public projections, dependency fakes, tool links, auth/policy subjects, and binary proxy fixtures now pass. |
| `c05-async-job-service` | `fixture-ready` | Lifecycle, idempotency, claim, heartbeat, log, complete, cancel, fail, and projection fixtures pass. |
| `c06-resource-lease-service` | `fixture-ready` | Lease lifecycle, local token facts, audit ownership, and deterministic timeout liveness pass. |
| `c07-artifact-store` | `fixture-ready` | Upload, content, completion, first-write validation, idempotency, upload-session read, and binary fixtures pass. |
| `c08-access-policy-and-secrets` | `fixture-ready` | Auth grammar, allow/deny decisions, supplied context, pre-artifact registration, and secrets scope pass. |
| `c09-runtime-node-agent` | `not fixture-ready` | One mechanical fixture regression remains: stopped-service start response status drifted from documented `202` to JSON `200`. |
| `c10-comfyui-provider` | `fixture-ready` | Provider health, invoke, validation, timeout, content-ref, binary, and error fixtures pass. |
| `composition-runner` | `fixture-ready` | Claim-to-complete, independent C05/C06 lifetimes, failure branches, binary handoff, and audit exclusion pass. |

## Boundary Findings

- No actor reported needing sibling private state.
- Public actor context remains clean.
- C04, C05, C07, and C09 fixes from Run 022 mostly held under full bounded
  rerun.
- The only blocking finding is a local C09 JSON/prose mismatch introduced by
  the post-Run 022 patch.

## Remaining Finding

Run 023 is not fixture-ready because:

- C09 `service_start_accepted` is inconsistent. The C09 contract says a stopped
  service `POST /v1/node/services/svc_comfyui_gpu/start` returns HTTP `202`
  with `status: starting`, and the fixture README repeats `202`; the
  machine-readable fixture currently returns `status: 200`. This prevents
  deterministic replay.

Non-blocking C09 checks:

- `service_running` now returns HTTP `200`.
- Start idempotency with key, without key, and conflict are covered.
- Local endpoint-auth facts are bounded and explicitly do not make C09 the
  owner of global policy rules.
- Provider boundary hygiene is clean.

## Outcome

Not fixture-ready. Nine actor packages passed; C09 has one local fixture
status-code regression.

At initial run recording time, no post-Run 023 patch had been applied yet. The
next step is to validate the proposed fix before editing role packages or
fixtures.

## Proposed Post-Run 023 Fix Batch

The coordinator proposes a narrow mechanical patch that:

- Restores C09 `service_start_accepted.response.status` to `202`.
- Keeps C09 `service_running.response.status` at `200`.
- Adds a process guardrail requiring fixture-ID-specific assertions after JSON
  fixture edits, so broad status or text searches do not miss wrong-fixture
  edits.

This proposal requires read-only constraint validation before patching.

## Validator Decision

Read-only constraint validator:
`019e9aed-c9f1-7d81-bb44-9f354e081162`.

Decision: `approved-with-notes`.

Notes:

- The C09 fixture correction is compliant: `service_start_accepted` should use
  HTTP `202` because both the C09 contract excerpt and fixture README define
  stopped-service start as accepted/starting.
- Keeping `service_running.response.status` at `200` preserves the prior Run
  022 fix and matches the service-status contract.
- The fixture-ID-specific assertion guardrail is appropriate process hardening
  and does not weaken component isolation or introduce hidden shared state.
- This approval does not make S003 fixture-ready by itself. Run 024 must rerun
  the full bounded scenario after the patch before any fixture-ready acceptance
  claim.

## Post-Run 023 Patch

The approved patch was applied locally after validation:

- C09 `service_start_accepted.response.status` was restored to `202`.
- C09 `service_running.response.status` remains `200`.
- `AGENTS.md`, `Learnings.md`, and `blog.md` now record the
  fixture-ID-specific assertion guardrail.

This is improvement work, not fixture-ready evidence. Run 024 must prove the
updated packages.
